RESPONSES TO PLTFS RESPONSES TO SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS IN SUPPORT OF SUMMAYR JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT INDEPENDENT TELEVISION SERVICE, INC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INDEPENDENT TELEVISON SERVICES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ASSUMING DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION, THE SUN DECLARATION CREATES TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE TERMS OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT, SUN'S AUTHORITY TO NEGOTIATE ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT AND SUN'S COMPENSATION. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE SUN DECLARATION 16, 25, AND 27 ARE SUSTAINED; THE REMAINDER ARE OVERRULED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE FRAUD CLAIM IS GRANTED. THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION WAS SHIFTED AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE OF A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INDEPENDENT TELEVISION SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. AS TO THE QUANTUM MERUIT CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT PLAINTIFF'S SERVICES BENEFITTED DEFENDANT. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE BUSINESS ADVANTAGE, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS WRONGFUL BY SOME LEGAL MEASURE OTHER THAN THE FACT OF INTERFERENCE ITSELF, OR THAT DEFENDANT WAS AN OUTSIDER THAT INTERFERED WITH A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP THAT PLAINTIFF HAD WITH JETSON/LNTV. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INDEPENDENT TELEVISION SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Jordan A. Sussman, Esq. of Downing Law Firm (415) 986-3644, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; David Sohn, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins (415) 442-4810, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication is granted. As to the quantum meruit cause of action, plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact that plaintiff's services benefitted defendant. As to the cause of action for intentional interference with prospective business advantage, plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact that defendant's conduct was wrongful by some legal measure other than the fact of interference itself, or that defendant was an outsider that interfered with a contractual relationship that plaintiff had with Jetson/LNTV. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INDEPENDENT TELEVISON SERVICES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Douglas Farmer of Ogletree Deakins (442-4810) for defendant and moving party
Arthur Liu of Inter-Pacific Law Group Incorporated (510-986-1198) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: motion for summary judgment is denied. Assuming defendant has shifted the burden of production, the Sun declaration creates triable issues of fact as to the terms of the parties' agreement, Sun's authority to negotiate on behalf of defendant and Sun's compensation. Defendant's objections to the Sun declaration 16, 25, and 27 are sustained; the remainder are overruled. The motion for summary adjudication of the fraud claim is granted. The burden of production was shifted and plaintiff failed to present evidence of a triable issue of fact. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452
